title: Carson Atwood Website
text: My name is Carson Atwood, and I am a master’s student in the Department of Animal and Food Science at Oklahoma State University. My research focuses on broiler behavior and welfare.

title: Carson Atwood CV
text:
Education
Oklahoma State University, Stillwater, OK
Master of Science | Animal Science
Expected Graduation: December 2026
Focus: Broiler Chicken Welfare & Behavior
Advisor: Dr. Meaghan M. Meyer
Oklahoma State University, Stillwater, OK
Bachelor of Science | Animal Science
December 2024

title: Carson Atwood CV
text:
Experience
Oklahoma State University, Stillwater, OK *January 2025 – Present
Graduate Research Assistant
Graduate Research Assistant:

Quantifying social status in growing broiler chickens using Aggressive Rank Index (ARI)

Analyze video footage to quantify social interactions and dominance behaviors across ranks.
Calculate ranks to classify individual birds by social status.
Manage data entry, quality control, and statistical analysis using Excel, SAS, and R Studio.
Train and supervise 5 undergraduate researchers to ensure inter-observer reliability in behavior observations.

Evaluating social status effects on environmental enrichment use and welfare in growing broiler chickens

Evaluate the impact of social status on environmental enrichment usage, welfare, performance, and stress physiology in growing broiler chickens.
Oversee daily management and husbandry throughout the full broiler grow-out period.
Collect and record performance outcomes weekly.
Collect and record welfare outcomes weekly.
Coordinate and perform post-mortem biological sample collection.


Graduate Teaching Assistant:

Poultry Science

Co-instruct weekly laboratory sessions focused on applied broiler welfare, production, and management.
Supervise and coordinate the broiler grow-out project, ensuring proper husbandry, and welfare standards.
Guide students in accurate data collection and management.
Deliver guest lectures on core topics related to various poultry production topics.

Applied Animal Nutrition

Develop complete course materials, including lectures, assessments, and activities to enhance student comprehension and student engagement.
Assist in evaluating student performance by grading assignments, quizzes, and exams.
Create and administer course review sessions and maintain regular office hours to support student comprehension of course material.

Introduction to the Animal Science Lab

Independently instruct 2-3 laboratory sections each week covering foundational concepts in animal science.
Guide students through comparative production systems across various livestock species.
Implement interactive, hands-on activities to reinforce lecture material and enhance applied learning.

Capstone for Animal Agriculture

Grade and evaluate graduating seniors’ capstone research literature reviews and oral presentations.
Coordinate and assist with daily classroom activities, including organizing guest lectures and teaching laboratory sessions.



Gawf Farms, Eufaula, OK *October 2021 – December 2024
Manager

Oversee daily operations for a mixed-species livestock enterprise managing over 700 goats and cattle.
Design and implement feed, breeding, and herd-health programs to improve productivity and animal well-being.
Manage budgets, marketing, and record keeping ensuring operational efficiency and profitability.
Supervise employees and seasonal staff, ensuring adherence to welfare and biosecurity standards.


Oklahoma State University, Stillwater, OK *August 2024 – December 2024
Applied Animal Nutrition Undergraduate Teaching Assistant

Supported course coordination and communication between the professor and students.
Graded coursework and provided constructive feedback to enhance learning outcomes.


Oklahoma Animal Disease Diagnostic Laboratory, Stillwater, OK *September 2022 – October 2023
Necropsy & Receiving Assistant

Received and processed animal submissions for diagnostic testing, ensuring accurate chain-of-custody and sample labeling.
Collaborated with pathologists and veterinarians to support necropsy and sample preparation.
Organized case documentation and maintained biosecure laboratory environments.


Cattleman’s Congress, Oklahoma City, OK *December 2022 – January 2023
Livestock Intern

Assisted with the coordination and facilitation of livestock and exhibitor move-in and setup.
Maintained accurate records of class placings and managed award distribution logistics.
Enforced biosecurity protocols to ensure safe and efficient animal movement throughout the facility.


Oklahoma Animal Disease Diagnostic Laboratory, Stillwater, OK *March 2022 – November 2022
Histology Lab Technician

Prepared, processed, and embedded tissue samples for histopathological examination.
Assisted in slide preparation and labeling for diagnostic review.
Maintained laboratory organization and quality control procedures for sample integrity.

Atwood, C. D., J. L. Salak-Johnson, and M. M. Meyer. 2026. Social rank influences resource use and finishing growth performance in broilers. Poultry Science Association Annual Meeting, Toronto, Canada.
